question.text: Which option Greek is ALWAYS negative for a long option position (both calls and puts)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Theta (time decay) is always negative for long option positions because options lose value as time passes, all else being equal. **The Trap:** Delta is positive for long calls but negative for long puts. Vega and Gamma are positive for long options. **Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding the sign of Greeks for different positions is tested in NISM Equity Derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Theta works in favor of option sellers (short positions). If you are long options, time is your enemy. If you are short, time is your friend.
